prompt: Using RFdiffusion, design a de novo TIM-barrel enzyme (250–300 residues) that recapitulates the active-site geometry and substrate specificity of human glucocerebrosidase (GBA) for use as a synthetic replacement in enzyme replacement therapy for Gaucher disease. The designed enzyme should achieve kcat/Km ≥ 10^4 M⁻¹s⁻¹. outline a methodology for: (1) TIM-barrel scaffold backbone generation with RFdiffusion and active-site hallucination, (2) catalytic residue placement using ProteinMPNN with fixed-position constraints, (3) molecular dynamics simulation to assess active-site preorganisation. Include a discussion of glycosylation requirements for lysosomal targeting.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05); target: GBA glucocerebrosidase; organism: Homo sapiens; catalogue version v1.0
